priority.rule: For EVERY item extracted, use the highest available source. NEVER use a lower-priority source when a higher one exists.

levels.description: Tabular schedule listing items with quantities, specifications, and model numbers.
levels.reliability: Highest — schedules are prepared by the designer and are the definitive quantity/spec source.
levels.action: Use schedule quantity and specification directly. Cross-reference drawing for location verification.

levels.description: Dimensions or quantities explicitly written on the drawing as text annotations.
levels.reliability: High — figured dimensions are authoritative. They override scaled measurements.
levels.action: Use the stated value. Note: "figured dimensions always override scaled measurements".

levels.description: Quantity measured from the drawing using the stated scale or scale bar.
levels.reliability: Good — standard method for linear measurements when no annotation exists.
levels.action: Measure along service centreline using drawing scale. Note measurement method and scale used.
prerequisites: OR stated scale in title block with confirmed paper size
OR known grid dimensions for calibration
levels.note: If neither scale bar nor stated scale is available, DO NOT measure. Flag and extract counts only.

levels.description: Quantity estimated using standard allowance rates when items are not individually shown.
levels.reliability: Lowest — estimates are approximations based on industry averages.
levels.action: Apply allowance rates from KB-C02. ALWAYS flag as estimated. State the basis of estimate.
levels.warning: NEVER use estimated allowance when a schedule, annotation, or scale measurement is available. Estimated allowances are the LAST RESORT.

priority.validation_rule: After extraction, review every item. If an item is labelled "Estimated allowance" but a schedule or drawing measurement exists for that item, upgrade to the higher-priority source.

linear.name: Linear Measurement (m)
linear.applies_to: Pipework, cables, containment, circular duct, flexible duct, conduit, trunking
method: 1. IDENTIFY start point (source: DB, pump, boiler, header, etc.) and end point (load: radiator, socket, terminal device, etc.).
2. TRACE the service route along the centreline as shown on the GA drawing.
3. INCLUDE all horizontal runs along the route.
4. INCLUDE vertical drops and rises — from tray/ceiling level down to equipment, up through risers.
5. If vertical distance is not shown on plan, estimate from: floor-to-floor height (KB-C01), ceiling void depth, equipment connection height. Flag as "estimated vertical".
6. SUM all segments for total route length.
7. APPLY waste factor from KB-C02 (noted separately).
8. APPLY rounding rule from KB-C02 (round UP to nearest 0.5m).
do_not_measure_from: Schematics or flow diagrams — these show LOGIC, not physical routing. Lengths are diagrammatic.
Riser diagrams — these show vertical distribution schematically. Lengths are NOT to scale.
Detail drawings — these show installation method at enlarged scale. Not for quantity extraction.
Single-line diagrams — these show circuit topology, not physical cable routes.

vertical_estimation.rule: When vertical runs cannot be measured from section drawings, estimate using:
methods: Floor-to-floor height from architectural drawings or KB-C01 defaults (commercial 3.75m, residential 2.7m)
Ceiling void depth: typically 300-600mm above ceiling tiles
Equipment connection drop: typically 300-1500mm from tray/ceiling to equipment
Add 2× 90° elbows per vertical transition (fitting count)
vertical_estimation.label: Source: Estimated vertical — no section view provided.
vertical_estimation.cross_ref: KB-C01 for floor-to-floor defaults. KB-M02 for riser fittings allowance.

area.name: Area Measurement (m²)
area.applies_to: Rectangular ductwork, duct insulation, equipment insulation, flat insulation boards, fire stopping boards

rectangular_duct.note: W and H in metres. L = total centreline length.

circular_duct_surface.note: D in metres. For insulation measurement.

flat_oval_duct.note: H = minor axis, W = major axis.

cylinder_vessel.note: Vertical cylinder. Includes top and bottom.

rules: Include surface area at bends, tees, and fittings — add 10-15% for fittings.
For insulation: use EXTERNAL duct dimensions for external wrap, INTERNAL for internal lining.
Cladding m² = insulation m² + 5-10% for laps and joints.
If equipment dimensions are not available, flag "dimensions required — cannot calculate surface area".
area.cross_ref: KB-I02 for duct insulation formulas. KB-I03 for equipment insulation formulas.

count.name: Item Count (nr)
count.applies_to: Valves, fittings, equipment, luminaires, outlets, dampers, detectors, sensors
method: 1. COUNT from drawing — identify every instance of the item symbol on each drawing sheet.
2. CROSS-CHECK against schedule — if a schedule exists, the schedule count is authoritative.
3. If counts DIFFER between drawing and schedule, flag the discrepancy. Use the schedule count.
4. AVOID double counting — check that the same item is not shown on multiple overlapping drawings.
5. For items with different types/sizes, count EACH TYPE separately as a separate line item.
double_count_risks: Same item shown on both ceiling plan and floor plan — count ONCE.
Same item shown on both GA drawing and coordination drawing — count from GA only.
Equipment shown on plan AND on equipment schedule — use schedule count, verify against plan.
Items near drawing sheet boundaries — may appear on two adjacent sheets. Count ONCE.
Items in revision clouds on BOTH old and new revisions — count from latest revision only.

implicit_items.rule: Some items are ALWAYS required even if not shown on drawings. Include as "Source: Implicit" with Medium confidence.
items: Strainers upstream of control valves and pumps (KB-M02)
Drain points at system low points (KB-M02)
Air vents at system high points (KB-M02)
Fire stopping at every fire-rated wall/floor penetration (KB-I04)
implicit_items.cross_ref: KB-M02 for implicit mechanical items. KB-I04 for fire stopping.

multi_floor.rule: When extracting from multiple drawings (multi-floor building), track quantities by floor AND by system. Provide both per-floor and total summaries.
process: 1. ASSIGN each drawing to a floor or area (e.g. "Ground Floor", "Level 01", "Roof Plant").
2. EXTRACT quantities per drawing/floor, keeping each floor's quantities separate.
3. IDENTIFY riser services that cross floor boundaries — measure vertical runs separately.
4. SUM per-floor quantities to get building totals.
5. PRESENT results as: per-floor breakdown + building total.

floor_assignment.from_title_block: Drawing title usually states the floor: "Level 03 — HVAC Layout", "Ground Floor Lighting".

riser_tracking.rule: Risers (vertical service runs between floors) must be tracked separately from floor-level distribution.
measurement: If riser diagram provided: identify services in riser, note sizes. Do NOT measure lengths from riser diagram (it is schematic).
Calculate riser length: number of floors served × floor-to-floor height.
Add fittings: 2× elbows at base (horizontal to vertical), 2× elbows at top, 1× fire stop per floor penetration.
Add isolation: 1× isolation valve per floor landing (if specified).
riser_tracking.cross_ref: KB-C01 for floor-to-floor height defaults. KB-M02 for riser fittings. KB-I04 for fire stopping.

similar_floors.rule: If multiple floors are identical (typical floors), measure ONE floor in detail and multiply by the number of typical floors.
similar_floors.check: Verify that floors are truly identical — check drawing titles for "Typical Floor" or "Levels 02-08 Typical".
similar_floors.differences: Even "typical" floors may differ at: riser connections (different branch sizes per floor), fire compartmentation (different at some levels), end floors (top/bottom may differ).
similar_floors.flag_if: Drawing says "typical" but floor-specific details suggest differences — flag for clarification.

extract.label: EXTRACT — include in takeoff
extract.criteria: Item is clearly shown on a GA drawing (or schedule) with sufficient specification to identify it and a measurable/countable quantity.
examples: Pipe run shown on GA with annotated size and measurable route — extract length + size.
Diffuser shown on RCP and listed on luminaire schedule — extract count + type.
Cable tray shown on GA with annotated width — extract length + width.
Valve shown on drawing at identified location with size annotation — extract as nr + size.

extract_and_flag.label: EXTRACT but FLAG — include in takeoff with flag
extract_and_flag.criteria: Item is shown/countable but has incomplete specification or measurement uncertainty.

scenarios.example: Pipe visible on drawing but material not annotated — extract length, flag "material not stated".
Length measured using title block scale (no bar) — extract with note "measured from stated scale, no scale bar".
Route shown on coordination drawing — extract as PRELIMINARY, flag "For Coordination — may change".
Isolation valves at pump connections (always required but not drawn) — extract as nr, flag "implicit — verify".
Fittings estimated at 1 per 3m (not individually shown) — extract estimated count, flag "estimated allowance".
Item within revision cloud on For Construction drawing — extract but flag "recently revised — verify current".

flag_no_extract.label: FLAG but DO NOT EXTRACT — note in flags section only
flag_no_extract.criteria: Item exists on drawings but cannot be reliably quantified. Or item is on a drawing type that does not support quantity extraction.

scenarios.reason: Schematics show logic, not physical routing. Linear quantities would be fictitious.

scenarios.scenario: Item on riser diagram without dimensions
scenarios.reason: Riser diagrams are not to scale. Measure risers from floor-to-floor heights instead.
Details show installation method, not quantities. Unless detail explicitly states a quantity.

scenarios.scenario: Drawing quality too poor to measure
scenarios.reason: Blurred, low resolution, or illegible drawing. Measurement would be unreliable.
Design is still changing. Quantities in this area are unreliable.
Cannot identify what it is. Note symbol and location, flag for identification.
Design not confirmed. Include as flag — do not extract quantity.

completeness_check.rule: Before finalising ANY extraction output, run this completeness check. Every extraction must pass ALL checks.

checks.question: Have Mechanical, Electrical, AND Insulation trades all been considered?
checks.action: If drawings show services for a trade not yet extracted, flag as "Trade not fully extracted — additional drawings may be required".
checks.note: Even if a drawing is primarily mechanical, check for electrical items (BMS points, power supplies to valves) and insulation items (pipe insulation, fire stopping).

checks.question: Has every drawing sheet provided been reviewed?
checks.action: List all drawing numbers processed. Flag any drawing sheets mentioned in the set that were not provided.
checks.note: Multi-sheet drawings may have a drawing index on Sheet 1 — check against it.

checks.question: Has the drawing title block information been captured?
checks.action: Project name, drawing number, revision, scale, status, originator — all should be in the output metadata.
checks.cross_ref: KB-C01 for title block field list.

checks.question: Has the drawing status been checked and flagged if not For Construction?
checks.action: If drawings are Preliminary, For Coordination, or Tender status — all quantities should be flagged as PRELIMINARY.
checks.cross_ref: KB-C01 for status hierarchy. KB-C04 for quantity status classification.

checks.question: Was a legend/key used for symbol identification? If no legend exists, was this flagged?
checks.action: If no legend: flag prominently. All symbol identifications are assumptions.

checks.question: Have waste factors been noted SEPARATELY for every linear/area quantity?
checks.action: Waste factors from KB-C02 should appear as separate notes or columns — NEVER hidden inside the measured quantity.

checks.question: Is the flags section present and populated?
checks.action: Every extraction MUST have a flags section. If nothing to flag, state "No flags identified". An empty flags section suggests incomplete review.
checks.note: It is VERY rare to have zero flags. A clean "no flags" output should itself be verified.

checks.question: Have implicit items (always required but not drawn) been included?
checks.action: Check KB-M02 implicit items list, KB-I04 fire stopping at penetrations, KB-E03 earthing.
checks.cross_ref: KB-M02 for implicit mechanical. KB-I04 for fire stopping. KB-E03 for earthing.

checks.question: Does every extracted item have a confidence score (High/Medium/Low)?
checks.action: No item should lack a confidence assessment. Score per KB-C02 confidence rules.

checks.question: Is every item labelled with its source (Schedule/Drawing/Scale/Estimated/Implicit)?
checks.action: Every line item must state where the quantity came from. This is essential for audit trail.
checks.cross_ref: Section 1 of this module for source labels.

checks.question: Is every quantity classified as Firm, Preliminary, Tender, or Estimated?
checks.action: Based on drawing status per KB-C04 quantity status classification.

checks.question: Have related items across trades been cross-referenced?
checks.action: Electrical power to mechanical equipment, insulation to pipework, fire stopping at penetrations, BMS points at mechanical plant. These are common cross-trade items.
examples: Pump extracted (mechanical) → power supply extracted (electrical) → pipe insulation extracted (insulation) → fire stopping at wall (insulation)
AHU extracted → ductwork → duct insulation → fire dampers → electrical supply → BMS points

completeness_check.final_statement: After passing all checks, append to output: "Completeness check: PASSED. [N] items extracted across [trades]. [N] flags raised. KB v[version]."
